nextSession.thesis: This is the MONDAY 7/13 morning window and the cash session is OPEN (marketClosed=false) on a FRESH distinct bar — the first genuine run-over-run delta since Friday 7/10's close (the ~10 weekend reruns were all ONE observation under the distinct-bar rule). The tape is a mild broad pullback inside an intact bull: SPY 753 (−0.26% vs Fri 754.95), QQQ 718.33 (−1.0%, back to hug its 50-day ~716), VIX 16.44 (+1.41 off the 15.03 low — complacency EASING but still under the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so NOT a flip). The one real rotation change: XLF flipped Leading⚠Peaking (accel −0.57), leaving NO clean Leading sector. Monday is a SETUP day (no scheduled major release) — the job is to position the intact bull core BEFORE Tuesday 7/14's June CPI + the heavy big-bank open, hold cooling tech/semis without chasing, take the tool-aligned XLI−XLC rotation only on confirmation, and carry defined-risk into the catalyst cluster.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ / SPMO / VGT core
actions.rationale: The core is still bull-stacked (20>50>200) with strong medium-term momentum (SPY mom126/252 +9.79/+21.66; QQQ mom63 +17.68). Monday's −0.26%/−1.0% dip is a pullback to support, not a structural break; QQQ is resting on its rising 50-day rather than losing it.
actions.trigger: Hold while SPY holds the rising 50-day ~741 and QQQ holds ~716; do not chase an opening bounce ahead of CPI.
actions.invalidation: A confirmed daily close below the SPY 50-day (~741) or a broad loss of the QQQ/VGT/SPMO bull-stacks.

actions.subject: SPY / QQQ defined-risk protection into CPI
actions.rationale: VIX 16.44 is still low into a dense catalyst cluster (7/14 CPI colliding with the JPM/WFC/BAC/C/GS pre-open, PPI 7/15, retail+claims 7/16, 7/17 OPEX). With spot near the highs and short-vol crowded, pre-event defined-risk is cheaper than reacting to a gap.
actions.structuralAnchor: Extended bull −0.61% off the 52-week high; VIX only +1.41 off a 7-print-decline low; weekend/pre-event gap risk unpriced.
actions.trigger: Refresh the option chain at the open (gamma/options labs — the STALE 7/6 walls are not tradable), then put on ONLY defined-risk protection before Tuesday's 8:30 ET CPI.
actions.invalidation: Skip the hedge if refreshed options already price an extreme event premium; remove it after the catalyst if SPY holds structure and IV normalizes.

actions.subject: Trim XLI into XLC
actions.rationale: The sector tool's own verdict is "Rotate toward XLC as XLI weakens." XLC is Improving ↑/into (accel +0.92) while XLI is the sharpest decelerator on the board (Weakening ↓/out, accel −2.46). This is a fresh handoff, so size it as a scale — XLC is still bear-stacked (−1.75% below its 200-day) and needs price confirmation.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLC RRG Improving ↑, accel +0.92, rsRatio 99.21 (below 100), 200-day −1.75%. XLI RRG Weakening ↓, accel −2.46, but still bull-stacked +9.95% over its 200-day.
actions.trigger: Trim XLI into Monday strength; add XLC only on an rsRatio cross back above 100 with XLC price reclaiming its 200-day.
actions.invalidation: Stop if XLI acceleration turns positive and re-enters Leading, or if XLC fails to hold the Improving quadrant / rejects its 200-day.

actions.subject: XLV
actions.rationale: Healthcare is Weakening ↓/out with the second-sharpest negative acceleration (−1.64) yet still bull-stacked (+7.65% over its 200-day) — that combination makes strength an opportunity to reduce, not a base to add.
actions.structuralAnchor: XLV RRG Weakening ↓, rotation out, accel −1.64; bull-stack, 200-day +7.65%.
actions.trigger: Trim into Monday strength while the RRG stays Weakening and acceleration stays negative.
actions.invalidation: Stop trimming if XLV acceleration turns positive and it re-enters Leading with improving relative strength.

dataAsOf.bars: 2026-07-13 morning (Tier-A refresh 2026-07-13T15:03:05.943Z, 263 series). Cash session OPEN. This is a FRESH distinct market bar — the FIRST genuine run-over-run delta since Friday 7/10's close (SPY 754.95, VIX 15.03); the ~10 weekend reruns on that close are ONE observation under the distinct-bar rule, so change-detection is now real (2nd distinct observation). Deltas vs Fri 7/10: SPY 754.95−753 (−0.26%), QQQ 725.51−718.33 (−1.0%, pulled back to the 50-day ~716), SPMO 153.75−151.14, VGT 118.08−116.99 — a mild BROAD pullback, all still bull-stacked. SPY 753 (bull-stack 20>50>200, −0.61% off the 52-week high ~757.6, +8.88% over a rising 200-day ~691.6, +1.65% over the 50-day ~740.8; mom126/252 +9.79% / +21.66%), QQQ 718.33 (bull-stack, +0.36% over the 50-day ~715.7; mom5 −0.62, mom21 +3.67, mom63 +17.68), SPMO 151.14, VGT 116.99 (both bull-stacked), MSFT 389.36 (BEAR-stack, −3.3% below its 50-day ~402.7, −11.53% below its 200-day ~440.1, −27.72% off its 52-week high ~538.7; mom5 +0.68, mom21 −2.01, mom63 +5.21 — a mild stabilization inside a downtrend). The 20/50/200-day MA + 126/252-day momentum slice drives the backdrop.

dataAsOf.macro: VIX 16.44 (Tier-A) — UP +1.41 from the 15.03 7/10 close, the first uptick after a 7-print decline. That is complacency EASING as the tape approaches the CPI-week cluster, but it stays BELOW the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so the regime is unchanged (positive-gamma / orderly), not flipped. Fear&Greed NOT fetched this run (composite score reads 0 only because Fear&Greed is null — VIX-only, not a neutral tape). VIX term structure NOT in the Tier-A slice — re-pull VIX9D/VIX3M live at the open. WTI CARRIED/STALE ~$71.5 (last web-verify; re-verify at the open) — USO (oil) is Mixed with mom21 −15.83, a softer-headline-CPI tail if crude keeps fading.
dataAsOf.events: Near-term calendar CARRIED from the prior runs' 7/11 web verification (BLS / FRED / Census / U.S. Treasury / CME FedWatch / Kalshi); NO fresh web re-verification injected into Tier-A this run — dates are structural/confirmed, consensus print figures remain ESTIMATES, RE-VERIFY each at release. THE WEEK AHEAD leads: June CPI Tue 7/14 8:30am ET (BLS) is the marquee catalyst and COLLIDES with a HEAVY Q2 big-bank open — JPMorgan + Wells Fargo + Bank of America + Citigroup + Goldman Sachs ALL pre-open (~7am ET) the SAME morning (Morgan Stanley Wed 7/15); June PPI Wed 7/15 8:30am; June retail sales + weekly initial jobless claims Thu 7/16 8:30am; monthly OPEX Fri 7/17. Then the 7/28-29 FOMC (decision Wed 7/29 2:00pm, a non-SEP meeting — no new dot plot) collides with the MSFT FY26 Q4 print AMC 7/29; July NFP 8/7. FOMC path (CARRIED, CME FedWatch / Kalshi 7/11): ~79% HOLD / ~2% cut / ~15-18% HIKE tail — funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts, Chair Warsh hawkish; a hot print re-arms the hike tail rather than merely delaying cuts.

fearGreed.band: not fetched this run (VIX-only composite — refresh live, do not assume a band)

vix.term: structure NOT in the Tier-A slice — re-pull VIX9D/VIX3M live. VIX 16.44 is +1.41 off the 15.03 (7/10) low — the FIRST uptick after a 7-print decline (16.9↓16.74↓16.57↓16.05↓16.04↓15.84↓15.03↓ then 16.44↑). Complacency is EASING into the CPI-week cluster, but +1.41 is UNDER the 3-pt regime-change threshold, so this is still positive-gamma / orderly, not a flip. The crowd is a touch less offside than Friday but still short-vol into KNOWN events. Falsifier: a VIX push >19-20 or IVTS crossing into backwardation (>1.0).

breadth.note: STRUCTURALLY BROAD but leadership breadth is COOLING and there is NO clean Leading sector this bar. 8 of 11 sectors are bull-stacked (20>50>200); XLC is bear-stacked (−1.75% below its 200-day), XLE is tangled, XLY is tangled (−0.1%). The tool-aligned RRG (2-week RS accel): rotation INTO XLC (Improving ↑, accel +0.92) and XLE (Improving ↑, accel +0.68); rotation OUT / Weakening ↓ for XLK (accel −0.03), XLV (−1.64) and XLI (−2.46, the sharpest on the board); and the notable run-over-run change — XLF FLIPPED from Leading to Peaking ⚠ (rotation out, accel −0.57), so the board has NO Leading sector. The structural RS leader remains XLK (3m/6m RS +17.79/+17.82, +20.3% over its 200-day) but it is cooling. The strongest positive acceleration on the board is the MAGS complex (Improving ↑/into, accel +1.53) even though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked. Lagging/neutral: XLY, XLP, XLB, XLRE, XLU. Proxy from sector-member bars, not an exchange breadth feed. Author rotation from RRG state/accel, NOT the raw 1-month RS level.

regime.note: STRUCTURE FIRST: a healthy but EXTENDED bull near all-time highs, now on a FRESH distinct bar (Mon 7/13, cash open) that finally lets change-detection work vs Friday's 7/10 close. SPY 753 bull-stacked (20>50>200), −0.61% off its 52-week high, +8.88% over a rising 200-day, 126/252-day momentum +9.79% / +21.66%. The run-over-run delta is a MILD BROAD PULLBACK (SPY −0.26%, QQQ −1.0% back to its 50-day) with VIX ticking up +1.41 to 16.44 — complacency easing, not a regime flip (under the 3-pt threshold). Three features sit ON TOP of the intact bull. (1) A tool-aligned LEADERSHIP shift with NO clean Leading sector: the structural leaders TECH and SEMIS keep cooling on the 2-week accel — XLK Weakening ↓/out (accel −0.03) and SOXX Weakening ↓/out (accel −0.23) despite bull-stacks and the board's strongest 3-/6-month RS — and financials, the prior lone Leader, FLIPPED to Peaking ⚠ (XLF accel −0.57). Rotation INTO is early-stage and structurally weak (XLC Improving but bear-stacked; XLE Improving but tangled); the MAGS complex shows the strongest positive acceleration (+1.53). (2) The one genuine STRUCTURAL crack, MSFT — bear-stacked, −27.72% off its high, −11.53% below its 200-day — is stabilizing at the margin (mom5 +0.68, mom63 +5.21) but still idiosyncratic while QQQ/VGT/SPMO stay bull-stacked. (3) Real assets show NO haven bid: GLD Downtrend (15.8, mom63 −15.74), SLV washed out (0), BTC Downtrend (39.4, mom63 −21.48) — copper (CPER 55) and agriculture (DBA 55) are the only firm real-asset trends, a risk-on / disinflationary-at-the-margin backdrop. PERSISTENCE-GATE caveat: read tech/semis "out" as HOLD-don't-chase, not sell — the bull-stacks + 3/6-month RS are intact. All under a HAWKISH-HOLD Fed (7/29 ~79% hold / ~15-18% HIKE, no 2026 cuts). The week: Mon 7/13 setup (open) − CPI + heavy bank open 7/14 − PPI 7/15 − retail + claims 7/16 − OPEX 7/17 − FOMC + MSFT 7/29. Posture: STAY LONG the structural bull; HOLD (don't chase) cooling tech/semis; take the tool-aligned XLI−XLC rotation only on confirmation; TRIM the rolling-over XLV; carry a defined-risk hedge INTO the CPI cluster; do NOT call an MSFT bottom until it reclaims its 50-day (~403).

SPY 753 is bull-stacked (20>50>200), −0.61% off the 52-week high, +8.88% over a rising 200-day; QQQ/SPMO/VGT bull-stacked. Leadership is rotating UNDER an intact index: tech/semis cooling on the 2-week accel, no clean Leading sector, MAGS turning up. The single structural exception is MSFT (bear-stack, −27.72% off high).

SPMO +1.44% / VGT +1.38% over their 50-days. Sector breadth 8/11 bull-stacked and above their 200-days. SOXX 12/12 above the 200-day, +46.4% over it. backdrop.macroCycle: HAWKISH-HOLD Fed: funds 3.50-3.75% held four straight meetings, no 2026 cuts priced, Chair Warsh hawkish. 7/29 FOMC (non-SEP) ~79% hold / ~2% cut / ~15-18% HIKE tail (CARRIED, CME FedWatch / Kalshi 7/11). A hot 7/14 CPI re-arms the hike tail rather than merely delaying cuts — the asymmetry the short-vol tape is underpricing. WTI carried/STALE ~$71.5; USO Mixed with mom21 −15.83 — softer crude is a softer-headline-CPI tail if it holds.
globalBackdrop: GLOBAL ROTATION is Asia-led and confirms the onshore semis / AI-hardware supply chain: EWT (Taiwan) leads at 73.9/100 vs ACWI (rel63 +25.74, rel126 +48.43), EWS (Singapore) 73.2 next, then EWC 57.9, EWY 57.4, EWJ 56, EWU 53.3 — but EVERY leader carries an FX headwind, so the equity strength is local-currency, not a weak-dollar tailwind (deep-link: global-rotation-lab.html).

AI-CAPEX CYCLE is the dominant secular driver — MSFT's structural breakdown (bear-stack, −27.72% off its high) vs the AI-hardware/semis bull-stacks (VGT/SPMO near highs, SOXX +46.4% over its 200-day, AMD mom63 +123, MU mom63 +124) IS the 'show-me' (software) vs 'build-it' (hardware/semis) dispersion at the single-name level. The MSFT capex guide 7/29 is the tell (deep-link: ai-capex-strategy-lab.html).

REAL ASSETS show NO haven bid: GLD Downtrend (15.8, mom63 −15.74), SLV washed out (0, mom63 −24, vol 76.5), BTC/IBIT Downtrend (39.4 / 33.5). Copper (CPER 55) and agriculture (DBA 55) are the only firm real-asset trends; the broad basket (DBC 40.4 / PDBC 40.8) and oil (USO 26.9) are Mixed. Net: risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin (deep-link: real-assets-lab.html).
backdrop.pricedIn: A benign-to-in-line CPI and an orderly bank open are the base case the short-vol tape (VIX 16.44) is positioned for — the ~47% "in-line" scenario. What is NOT priced: the weekend-into-CPI gap, a hot print that re-arms the ~15-18% hike tail, and any bank-earnings read-through that confirms/denies the XLF Peaking flip.
backdrop.asymmetry: Skewed to the downside into 7/14: spot is −0.61% off the high with VIX only 16.44, so a hot CPI or a bad bank open has more room to move price than an in-line print (already largely discounted). Upside case (cool CPI) lets the cooling leaders re-accelerate and fades the hike tail.
backdrop.whatWouldChangeIt: A confirmed SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ losing ~716; VIX pushing >19-20 or the term structure inverting; XLF's Peaking flip confirmed by a rejected bank-earnings open; or MSFT reclaiming its 50-day (~403) to end the lone structural downtrend. A hot CPI is the single fastest regime-changer given the hawkish-hold hike tail.

psychology.read: NAMED REGIME: a calm, positive-gamma, complacency-EASING tech-led bull with COOLING leadership and NO clean Leading sector. The crowd's posture is constructive-but-crowded — short-vol (VIX 16.44, only +1.41 off a 7-print-decline low), long the intact bull-stacks, and positioned for an in-line CPI (~47%). The fresh 7/13 bar shows the FIRST cracks in the complacency: a mild broad pullback (SPY −0.26%, QQQ −1.0% to its 50-day), VIX ticking up, and XLF rolling from the lone Leader to Peaking⚠ right before the bank open. This is the classic pre-catalyst setup — the tape is orderly because dealer gamma is positive and no one has been forced to hedge, NOT because the risk is small. What would FALSIFY the read: a VIX push >19-20 or term-structure inversion (regime flip), an SPY close below the 50-day (~741) or QQQ losing ~716 (structural break), a hot CPI re-arming the hike tail (the biggest single falsifier), or MAGS broadening + tech re-accelerating (bull re-leads and the cooling was just a pause).
inputs: FRESH distinct bar vs Fri 7/10 close: SPY −0.26%, QQQ −1.0% (to its 50-day), VIX 15.03−16.44 (+1.41, under the 3-pt regime-change threshold) — mild broad pullback + complacency easing, not a regime flip
RRG rotation: NO clean Leading sector; XLF flipped Leading⚠Peaking (accel −0.57); XLK/SOXX Weakening/out; XLC/XLE Improving/into (early, structurally weak); MAGS strongest positive accel (+1.53)
VIX 16.44, term structure not in the Tier-A slice (re-pull live); Fear&Greed not fetched (VIX-only composite)
Real assets: no haven bid — GLD 15.8 / SLV 0 / BTC 39.4 all Downtrend; CPER 55 & DBA 55 the only firm trends (risk-on, disinflationary-at-the-margin)
Catalyst cluster: 7/14 CPI + heavy bank open, 7/15 PPI+MS, 7/16 retail+claims, 7/17 OPEX, 7/29 FOMC+MSFT — under a hawkish-hold Fed (~79% hold / ~15-18% hike tail, carried)
psychology.tiltOnProbabilities: The base case remains bullish-to-neutral (in-line CPI ~47%, bull holds), but the fresh bar tilts probability weight toward the DOWNSIDE tail vs Friday: VIX +1.41, a broad pullback, and XLF's leadership roll all say the complacency is starting to price the catalyst. Net: same structural thesis, slightly higher hedge value into 7/14, and rotation calls taken only on confirmation (XLI−XLC) rather than anticipation.

groups.note: MAGS is Improving ↑/into with the board's strongest positive acceleration (+1.53) — the mega-cap complex turning UP on the 2-week accel even though only 1/7 members are bull-stacked (breadth 1/7). Leadership is narrow and top-heavy: AAPL at its 52-week high and META's +15.93 mom21 / +10.17 mom5 surge carry the complex, NVDA is bouncing (+6.07 mom5), while MSFT (the crack) and TSLA (−5.24 mom5) lag. An EARLY-accumulation signal under a weak-breadth surface — WATCH for broadening (>1/7 bull-stacked) to confirm, don't chase. Author from the RRG state/accel, not the raw 1-month RS.

groups.label: Semiconductors

notable.reason: strongest structural trend — mom63 +124, +101% over its 200-day
sharpest near-term fade — the group laggard to watch
bouncing but tangled — the mega-cap semis swing tell

groups.note: SOXX is Weakening ↓/out on the 2-week accel (−0.23, improving from deeper negatives) yet remains the strongest group structurally: 8/12 bull-stacked, 12/12 above the 200-day, +46.4% over its own 200-day, 3m/6m RS +34.81/+66.84. The cooling is decelerating momentum in an intact uptrend — HOLD, don't chase or sell. AMD (mom63 +123) and MU (mom63 +124) are the structural anchors; MRVL (mom21 −11.81) is the near-term fade. NVDA's bounce is the mega-cap tell for whether the group re-accelerates.

experimental.title: Rotation-flip detector (Leading⚠Peaking)
experimental.note: Method: flag any sector whose RRG state crosses from Leading to Peaking with negative acceleration on a fresh bar (this run: XLF accel −0.57), then event-study the forward 5/10-day sector return conditioned on whether the coincident earnings/catalyst confirmed or denied the roll. Inputs: per-sector rsRatio/rsMom/accel history + the sector's next scheduled catalyst. No owning tool captures the state-transition timing today.

experimental.title: Distinct-bar change-detection gate
experimental.note: Method: only compute run-over-run deltas across DISTINCT market-bar asOf dates (weekend/holiday reruns on one close = one observation), to stop manufacturing "changes" from identical carried closes. Inputs: bar asOf timestamps + a persistence counter. This run is the first genuine delta (7/13) after ~10 reruns on the 7/10 close.

experimental.title: RRG-state vs raw-RS-level divergence
experimental.note: Method: score sectors where the 2-week RS acceleration (RRG state) disagrees with the raw 1-month RS level (e.g. XLI: +2.87 1m RS but accel −2.46 / Weakening), and test which better predicts forward relative return. Inputs: rsMom1m vs accel per sector. The tool authors from state/accel; this quantifies whether that's the right choice.

experimental.title: Fresh-vs-entrenched rotation quality
experimental.note: Method: separate "rotation into" signals that are structurally confirmed (bull-stacked, above 200-day) from structurally weak ones (XLC Improving but bear-stacked −1.75% below its 200-day) and measure hit-rate difference. Inputs: rrgState + maStack + ma200Dist. Guards against chasing weak early-accumulation.

experimental.title: Complacency-into-catalyst hedge timer
experimental.note: Method: trigger a defined-risk hedge study when VIX sits in a low percentile AND term structure is in contango AND a Tier-1 catalyst (CPI/FOMC/NFP) is within N sessions; measure the hedge's expected value vs reacting post-event. Inputs: VIX level/percentile + VIX9D/VIX3M + the macro calendar. This run: VIX 16.44 into 7/14 CPI.

experimental.title: Mega-cap breadth-vs-accel early-turn signal
experimental.note: Method: flag groups (MAGS) where the ETF accel turns positive while member breadth is still weak (accel +1.53, breadth 1/7 bull-stacked), then test whether the accel or the breadth leads the forward move. Inputs: group ETF accel + member bull-stacked count. Distinguishes a real early turn from a top-heavy head-fake.

experimental.title: Hawkish-hold hike-tail pricing monitor
experimental.note: Method: track the CME FedWatch / Kalshi implied hike probability into each CPI/PPI/NFP and event-study equity/duration reaction conditioned on whether the print re-arms vs fades the hike tail (a different regime than the usual delay-the-cuts read). Inputs: implied path + realized macro surprise. Carried: ~15-18% hike tail, no 2026 cuts.

experimental.title: No-haven-bid risk-on confirmer
experimental.note: Method: confirm a risk-on tape by requiring the real-asset havens (GLD/SLV/BTC) to be trend-DOWN while cyclical real assets (CPER/DBA) firm — a cross-asset check that the equity bull is not masking a defensive rotation. Inputs: real-assets-lab model scores/trends. This run: GLD/SLV/BTC all Downtrend, CPER/DBA Uptrend = confirmed risk-on.
